description: ¬ß31 E-DG-001 POSITIVE ‚Äî a cycle in the dependency graph's `awaits` edges. `loadOwner` and `loadPet` each touch the db (`?{`‚Ä¶`}`), so each auto-escalates to server under the ¬ß12.2 T1 escalation trigger; a call to a server-escalated function is an `awaits` (sequencing) edge per ¬ß13.2. `loadOwner` calls `loadPet` and `loadPet` calls back to `loadOwner`, so the awaits subgraph contains a cycle and no valid await ordering exists ‚Äî neither operation can start. E-DG-001 fires AS an error (¬ß34: Severity Error), blocking codegen. This is the `awaits`-edge cycle specifically ‚Äî distinct from the derived-CELL cycle (E-DERIVED-CIRCULAR-DEP, ¬ß6.6.10 / ¬ß31.5) and the derived-ENGINE cycle (E-DERIVED-ENGINE-CIRCULAR, ¬ß51.0.J). Incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED / W-SQL-ROW-UNTYPED ignored by superset. Paired with dg-001-cyclic-neg (byte-identical but for `loadPet`'s back-call).
rationale: ¬ß31.1: the dependency graph is the shared source of truth for async scheduling ‚Äî 'determines what can parallelize vs. what must sequence (Section 13)'. ¬ß31.2: 'The graph SHALL represent all data dependencies: every operation that consumes the output of another operation is a dependent edge.' ¬ß13.2: the compiler SHALL 'Sequence dependent operations using `await` in generated code' ‚Äî so a server‚Üíserver call is an awaits edge (¬ß13.4: 'A server-escalated function MAY call another server-escalated function'). A cycle in that subgraph admits no sequencing: the ¬ß34 catalog row (E-DG-001 | ¬ß31 | 'Dependency graph: circular dependency in reactive graph') makes it an Error and the program is rejected.
